hero.headline: Amrita Online MBA in Business Analytics: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #8 University 2025 | Grant Thornton co-branded | WES recognised

sections.tldr: Amrita Online MBA in Business Analytics is co-branded with Grant Thornton and covers advanced ML, deep learning, generative AI, data analytics over cloud, and data visualisation. Total tuition Rs 2,24,000 (semester fee Rs 56,000). Plus Rs 700 registration and Rs 11,000 exam fees. Total cost of programme Rs 2,35,700. Zero-cost EMI Rs 9,792/month directly through the university. NAAC A++. NIRF #8 University 2025. WES recognised.

about.body:
Business Analytics is the fastest-growing MBA specialisation in India as organisations in every sector build analytics functions to support decision-making. Amrita's MBA in Business Analytics is co-branded with Grant Thornton - a Big-6 global advisory firm - making it one of the very few online MBA programmes in India where the curriculum and certifications carry a leading advisory firm's name alongside the university degree.

The curriculum covers advanced machine learning, deep learning fundamentals, generative AI, data analytics over cloud platforms, and data visualisation and communications. The Grant Thornton co-branding means graduates receive certifications from both Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham and Grant Thornton, adding advisory-firm credibility to the analytics credential.

Amrita holds NIRF #8 University 2025 and NAAC A++ - India's highest accreditation grade. WES recognition gives the degree formal equivalence to a Canadian and American master's degree. The Study Abroad programme offers exchange at Oakland University (USA), University of New Mexico (USA), Institut Mines Telecom (France), and University of Barcelona (Spain).

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amrita MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the Business Analytics specialisation.

subjects.name: International Business
subjects.desc: Global trade frameworks, cross-border market entry strategies, FDI analysis, and managing international operations.

subjects.name: Business Analytics and Business Modelling
subjects.desc: Quantitative business modelling, regression analysis, forecasting techniques, and data-driven decision-making frameworks.

subjects.name: Advanced Machine Learning
subjects.desc: Ensemble methods, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and advanced ML pipeline design for business analytics applications.

subjects.name: Deep Learning Fundamentals
subjects.desc: Neural network design, activation functions, backpropagation, and deep learning application areas in business analytics.

subjects.name: Education for Life
subjects.desc: Soft skills development, mindfulness, health and wellbeing, and holistic personal effectiveness for professionals.

subjects.name: Environment Sustainability and Governance
subjects.desc: ESG reporting frameworks, corporate sustainability strategy, carbon accounting basics, and governance obligations.

subjects.name: Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry analytics project integrating Business Analytics specialisation knowledge with real-world business problem-solving.

subjects.name: Data Visualization and Communications
subjects.desc: Dashboard design principles, executive reporting, Power BI and Tableau, and translating model outputs into business decisions.

subjects.name: Data Analytics over Cloud
subjects.desc: Cloud data platform basics (AWS, GCP, Azure), big data processing frameworks, and cloud analytics pipeline design.

subjects.name: Generative AI
subjects.desc: Generative AI model types, LLM application in business analytics, prompt engineering, and responsible AI governance.

whoHires.body:
Business Analytics MBA graduates from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online are hired across India's data-driven industries:

**Industries:** IT services (analytics practices), BFSI analytics, e-commerce, FMCG analytics, consulting firms, healthcare analytics, edtech, and analytics-first startups

**Entry-level roles:** Business Analyst, Data Analyst, BI Developer, Analytics Associate, MIS Executive, Decision Science Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Senior Business Analyst, Analytics Manager, BI Manager, Data Science Manager, Analytics Lead, Decision Science Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Analytics, Head of Data Science, Chief Data Officer, Director of Business Intelligence, Analytics Director (consulting), VP Decision Sciences

**Top hiring organisations:** TCS Analytics, Infosys BPM, Accenture Analytics, KPMG Data and Analytics, Deloitte Insights, Grant Thornton Advisory (analytics practice), HDFC Bank analytics, ICICI Bank data science, Flipkart analytics, Amazon India data, Zomato data, and analytics startups across Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, and Mumbai

skills.body:
The Business Analytics specialisation builds skills that analytics employers and Grant Thornton-affiliated advisory firms test at analyst and manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Machine Learning: advanced ensemble techniques (Random Forest, XGBoost, gradient boosting), model comparison frameworks, and production ML deployment planning
- Deep Learning: neural architecture selection, transfer learning, CNN for image data, and business framing for deep learning projects
- Cloud Analytics: AWS Redshift/Athena, Google BigQuery, Azure Synapse basics, cloud data pipeline design, and cost-benefit of cloud analytics platforms
- Generative AI: LLM prompt engineering for business analysis, RAG architecture basics, AI-assisted reporting, and GenAI risk assessment

**Applied skills:**
- Data Visualisation and Communications: executive dashboard design, storytelling with data, and communicating uncertainty in model outputs to business leaders
- Business Modelling: regression model interpretation, scenario analysis, sensitivity testing, and model assumption documentation for audit

**Soft skills:** Presenting analytics findings to non-technical C-suite stakeholders, managing data requests and prioritisation across multiple business teams, communicating model limitations and confidence intervals to decision-makers, and building data literacy in non-analytics teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amrita Online MBA Business Analytics against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Business Analytics:** CU at Rs 1,65,000 (Tier 1) offers NIRF #19, PwC co-teaching, and Harvard certificate alongside business analytics. CU is stronger for candidates wanting PwC credentials at a lower price. Amrita at Rs 2,24,000 adds NAAC A++ (vs A+), NIRF #8 (vs #19), Grant Thornton co-branding in analytics, and WES recognition - the premium is justified for candidates targeting advisory or international roles.

**vs LPU Online MBA Business Analytics:** LPU at NAAC A++ has a business analytics curriculum but without co-branding from a Big-6 advisory firm. At comparable or slightly higher fees, Amrita's Grant Thornton co-branded certification and NIRF #8 rank differentiate it for candidates targeting analytics consulting and BFSI analytics.

**vs UPES Online MBA Business Analytics:** UPES at NIRF #45 and NAAC A has a functional analytics curriculum at Rs 1,75,000. Amrita at Rs 2,24,000 with NAAC A++ and Grant Thornton co-branding is positioned at a higher tier for candidates where institutional credibility and advisory-firm certification matter.

fees.body:
The Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA in Business Analytics carries a tuition fee of Rs 2,24,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme).

**Mandatory fees (all students, disclose before enrolment):**
- Registration fee: Rs 700 (one-time)
- Examination fee: Rs 2,750 per semester x 4 = Rs 11,000 total
- **Total cost of programme: Rs 2,24,000 + Rs 700 + Rs 11,000 = Rs 2,35,700**

**Payment options for Indian students:**
- Semester fee: Rs 56,000 per semester
- Annual fee with 5% discount: Rs 1,06,400 per year
- Zero-cost EMI: Rs 9,792/month directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs)

**Foreign national students:**
Contact our counsellor for foreign national tuition track applicable to the Business Analytics specialisation.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amrita each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham MBA degree certificate with Business Analytics noted on the marksheet, along with Grant Thornton co-branded certifications in analytics disciplines. The degree is issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 University 2025 are the strongest institutional credentials in India's online MBA space. WES recognition gives formal degree equivalence in the USA and Canada. DigiLocker verification is available for the Amrita degree.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA Business Analytics alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Suresh K.
items.liked: Grant Thornton certification alongside the Amrita degree was a genuine differentiator in analytics consulting interviews
items.disliked: Cloud analytics content (GCP, Azure) was covered at a conceptual level - hands-on lab access for cloud platforms would improve practical readiness
items.body: I work in analytics consulting and the Grant Thornton co-branding on the Business Analytics MBA opened doors that a standard online MBA would not. The advanced ML and generative AI subjects were current and directly applicable. NAAC A++ from NIRF #8 cleared the credential filter at every Big-4 and advisory firm I applied to. Zero-cost EMI at Rs 9,792/month made the fee structure very manageable alongside my existing salary.

items.name: Ananya P.
items.liked: Generative AI and Data Analytics over Cloud subjects were ahead of what most online MBA programmes offer
items.disliked: The proctoring system for online exams was occasionally glitchy - had to reschedule one exam session due to technical errors
items.body: I manage a business analytics team at a BFSI firm. The generative AI, cloud analytics, and deep learning fundamentals subjects were more current than I expected from an online MBA. Grant Thornton certification added advisory-firm credibility to my analytics profile. NAAC A++ carries real weight at BFSI employers - it cleared the management degree quality filter at my firm for the Analytics Manager designation.

items.name: Rahul V.
items.liked: Data Visualization and Communications subject directly improved my executive reporting and stakeholder communication skills
items.disliked: Placement assistance for Pune's analytics market was limited - most placement support seemed oriented toward Bengaluru and Chennai
items.body: I targeted the analytics consulting track and the Grant Thornton co-branding was the primary reason I chose Amrita over CU and LPU. The advanced ML and data visualisation subjects were directly applicable. WES recognition was a bonus - I have long-term plans for the Canadian market. NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 gave me confidence the credential would hold up at any employer filter.

faqs.question: What does the Grant Thornton co-branding mean for Business Analytics students?
faqs.answer: The Business Analytics MBA is co-branded with Grant Thornton - a Big-6 global advisory firm. Students receive certifications from both Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham and Grant Thornton as part of the programme. For analytics consulting, BFSI analytics, and advisory roles where a Big-6 firm's name on the credential adds hiring-round credibility, this is a genuine differentiator over standard university-only certifications.

faqs.question: What is the total cost of the Amrita Business Analytics MBA including all fees?
faqs.answer: Total cost of programme is Rs 2,35,700 - comprising Rs 2,24,000 tuition, Rs 700 one-time registration, and Rs 11,000 examination fees (Rs 2,750 per semester across 4 semesters). Zero-cost EMI is Rs 9,792/month directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham. Annual fee with 5% discount is Rs 1,06,400 per year.

faqs.question: Does the Business Analytics MBA cover generative AI and cloud analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sem 4 includes dedicated Generative AI and Data Analytics over Cloud subjects. Generative AI covers LLM application in business, prompt engineering, and responsible AI. Cloud Analytics covers AWS, GCP, and Azure data platform basics and pipeline design. These are among the most current subjects in any online MBA Business Analytics curriculum in India.

faqs.question: How does Amrita Business Analytics compare to CU's Business Analytics MBA?
faqs.answer: CU at Rs 1,65,000 (Tier 1) offers PwC co-teaching, Harvard certificate, and NIRF #19. Amrita at Rs 2,24,000 offers Grant Thornton co-branding in analytics, NIRF #8, NAAC A++ (vs CU's A+), and WES recognition. CU is stronger for price-sensitive candidates wanting PwC credentials. Amrita is stronger for candidates targeting analytics consulting or international roles where the higher NIRF rank, A++ grade, and WES recognition carry weight.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for a non-analytics professional transitioning into analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Sem 3 Business Analytics and Business Modelling subject builds quantitative foundations from scratch. The curriculum is framed around business applications of analytics - not data engineering. The Grant Thornton certification demonstrates advisory-standard analytics competency. The management core in Sem 1-2 ensures non-analytics professionals build cross-functional management literacy alongside the analytics specialisation.

chooseIf: You want Business Analytics MBA with Grant Thornton co-branding from NAAC A++, NIRF #8 institution.
WES recognition for USA and Canada career applications is a priority.
You want generative AI and cloud analytics in the curriculum alongside advanced ML.
Your target roles are in analytics consulting, BFSI analytics, or Big-4 advisory analytics practices.
consider: You want PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate at a lower price point (consider Chandigarh University Business Analytics).
Your budget is under Rs 2,00,000 and NAAC A++ and Grant Thornton branding are not filters for your target employers.
You want a broader tech-analytics curriculum (consider JAIN Business Intelligence and Analytics or UPES Business Analytics).
